title: Duplicate anonymous auth rule error message is missing a closing backtick
body: In AccessControl::new (src/auth.rs) the error for a bad anonymous paths value is anyhow!("Invalid auth value `@{paths}") -- the format string is missing the closing backtick before the format arg, so the rendered error looks broken, e.g. `Invalid auth value \`@/dir1:rw`. Minor but confusing when debugging --auth rules.

title: --auth-method digest nonce cache never expires expired entries proactively
body: auth.rs sets DIGEST_AUTH_TIMEOUT to 7 days and NONCESTARTHASH is seeded once per process, but I don't see anywhere that expired nonces get purged from memory between requests -- only checked at validation time. Under a long-running dufs instance serving many clients this could grow the in-memory nonce map unbounded.

title: --hidden glob patterns aren't documented to support directories vs files distinctly
body: The --hidden flag in args.rs says 'Hide paths from directory listings, e.g. tmp,*.log,*.lock' but it's unclear from --help alone whether 'tmp' hides only a top-level directory named tmp or any path component named tmp at any depth. Could use a clearer example in the help text.

title: DUFS_PORT env var silently ignored when --port also passed with an invalid value
body: Since 'port' uses value_parser!(u16) and env("DUFS_PORT"), passing an out-of-range --port like 99999 produces clap's generic parse error, but there's no indication in the message that DUFS_PORT could be the source if both are set. Consider clarifying precedence between CLI flag and env var in the error path.

title: WebDAV PROPFIND depth infinity not rejected, risking huge responses
body: tests/webdav.rs exercises PROPFIND handling but I don't see a depth-infinity guard in src/server.rs for very large directory trees -- a client sending Depth: infinity against a huge served tree could generate a massive XML response and block the async runtime while building it.
